description: COALESCE with a literal default makes the result non-nullable since the literal guarantees a non-NULL value

columns.name: bio
columns.note: COALESCE with literal default guarantees non-NULL

name: coalesce_nullable_with_default
query_sql:
-- @name GetUserWithDefaultBio
-- @returns :one
SELECT id, COALESCE(bio, 'N/A') AS bio FROM users WHERE id = $1
schema_sql: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT NOT NULL, bio TEXT, age INTEGER, score NUMERIC, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())

sqlc_comparison.scythe_improvement: scythe analyzes COALESCE arguments and recognizes that a literal default makes the result non-nullable
sqlc_comparison.sqlc_behavior: sqlc marks the COALESCE result as nullable because the first argument (bio) is nullable
